summary: Completed testing of TrustLens Dual Perspective / Mirror Mode feature. Backend: 8/8 tests passed via pytest. All mirror endpoints working correctly: POST /api/mirror/create, GET /api/mirror/{id}/join, GET /api/mirror/{id}/status, POST /api/mirror/{id}/consent, GET /api/mirror/{id}/report. Frontend: MirrorInvite page loads with welcome message and 'Begin My Analysis' button. DualReport page shows score rings, perception gap bars, AI narrative correctly after both partners consent. ResultsDashboard has 'Invite Your Partner for Mirror Analysis' section (data-testid=mirror-invite-section). Report includes all required fields: partner_a/partner_b scores, signals_detected, dominant_pattern, perception_gaps (5 dimensions), average_gap, gap_level, narrative.
passed_tests: Backend: POST /api/mirror/create creates mirror session and returns mirror_id
Backend: POST /api/mirror/create returns 404 for invalid session_id
Backend: GET /api/mirror/{id}/join creates new session for Partner B
Backend: GET /api/mirror/{id}/join returns already_joined=true if called again
Backend: GET /api/mirror/{id}/status returns correct flags (partner_b_joined, partner_b_complete, consent flags)
Backend: GET /api/mirror/{id}/status shows partner_b_joined=false initially, true after join
Backend: POST /api/mirror/{id}/consent sets consent for correct partner (role=a or role=b)
Backend: POST /api/mirror/{id}/consent returns both_consented=true after both consent
Backend: POST /api/mirror/{id}/consent returns 403 for invalid session
Backend: GET /api/mirror/{id}/report returns 403 if both partners have not consented
Backend: GET /api/mirror/{id}/report returns comparison report with all required fields after both consent
Backend: Report includes partner_a/partner_b with suspicion_score, suspicion_label, signals_detected, dominant_pattern
Backend: Report includes perception_gaps with 5 dimensions (emotional_distance, communication_quality, trust_level, behavioral_changes, intimacy)
Backend: Report includes average_gap, gap_level (aligned/moderate/significant), and narrative text
Frontend: /mirror-invite/{mirrorId} page loads with title 'Your partner invited you to a Mirror Analysis'
Frontend: MirrorInvite shows privacy explanations (3 cards: private, compare perspectives, control)
Frontend: MirrorInvite 'Begin My Analysis' button visible with data-testid='begin-mirror-analysis-btn'
Frontend: /dual-report/{mirrorId} shows full report with suspicion score rings for both partners
Frontend: DualReport shows signals detected cards for Partner A and Partner B
Frontend: DualReport shows perception gap analysis with bars for all 5 dimensions
Frontend: DualReport shows AI narrative from TrustLens Perspective section
Frontend: ResultsDashboard shows 'Invite Your Partner for a Mirror Analysis' section
Frontend: Solo analysis flow works (landing -> /clarity -> /analysis)
critical_code_review_comments: Backend server.py lines 1401-1427: POST /api/mirror/create correctly creates mirror session and tags Partner A's session with mirror_id and mirror_role
Backend server.py lines 1430-1463: GET /api/mirror/{id}/join creates new analysis session for Partner B, returns already_joined=true on repeat calls
Backend server.py lines 1466-1489: GET /api/mirror/{id}/status returns all required flags without exposing analysis data
Backend server.py lines 1492-1536: POST /api/mirror/{id}/consent correctly identifies partner by session_id, generates report when both consent
Backend server.py lines 1539-1558: GET /api/mirror/{id}/report correctly guards with 403 until both consent
Backend server.py lines 1561-1635: generate_dual_perspective_report computes suspicion scores, perception gaps for 5 dimensions, and AI narrative
Backend server.py lines 1638-1691: generate_dual_narrative uses Claude Sonnet 4.5 with fallback template
Frontend MirrorInvite.jsx: Uses data-testid attributes correctly for testing
Frontend DualReport.jsx: Shows waiting state with consent button when not both consented, full report when both consented
Frontend ResultsDashboard.jsx lines 978-1132: Mirror invite section with create-mirror-btn, consent buttons for both partners
seed_data_creation: Mirror sessions created during testing: mirror_id=0091ea17-7dd (both consented, report ready)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Mirror/Dual Perspective feature fully functional. Key test file: /app/backend/tests/test_mirror_quick.py. Pre-existing mirror session for testing: mirror_id=0091ea17-7dd. Backend API URL: https://signal-analysis-5.preview.emergentagent.com. AI narrative for dual report generated by Claude Sonnet 4.5 via EMERGENT_LLM_KEY with deterministic fallback.

mirror_endpoints.create: POST /api/mirror/create - creates mirror session with mirror_id
mirror_endpoints.join: GET /api/mirror/{id}/join - creates Partner B session, returns already_joined on repeat
mirror_endpoints.status: GET /api/mirror/{id}/status - returns partner_b_joined, partner_b_complete, consent flags, report_ready
mirror_endpoints.consent: POST /api/mirror/{id}/consent - sets consent for correct partner based on session_id
mirror_endpoints.report: GET /api/mirror/{id}/report - returns 403 until both consent, then returns full report

report_structure.narrative: AI-generated via Claude Sonnet 4.5 with fallback

frontend_pages.mirror_invite: /mirror-invite/{mirrorId} - Partner B welcome page with privacy explanations
frontend_pages.dual_report: /dual-report/{mirrorId} - Dual perspective comparison with score rings, gap bars, narrative
frontend_pages.results_dashboard: Mirror invite section with 'Generate Partner Invite' button
